## Resource

**Name:** <!-- Official project, paper, or article title (exact README link text) -->

**URL:** <!-- Canonical homepage, paper page, or primary article URL -->

**Section:** <!-- One of the ten README sections; see docs/taxonomy.md when section fit is unclear -->

## Why it belongs

Explain how this resource helps people manage **groups of agents** or their **flows** (for example coordination, orchestration, delegation, routing, handoffs, shared state, or group-level evaluation). Use factual, concise language.

## Checklist

Before requesting review, read [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md), [docs/taxonomy.md](docs/taxonomy.md), and [docs/review-policy.md](docs/review-policy.md). Run `make check` from the repository root; run `make links` when you add or change URLs.

- [ ] I added **one resource only** (no bundled entries or unrelated repository changes).
- [ ] This resource fits agent-factory scope: groups of agents, flows, orchestration, routing, handoffs, shared state, or group-level evaluation.
- [ ] The entry uses the required format: `- [Resource Name](URL) - Description.`
- [ ] The description is concise, factual, and non-promotional (encyclopedic tone).
- [ ] The description **ends with a period**.
- [ ] The entry is in **alphabetical order by link text** within its target section.
- [ ] The URL is **not already present** anywhere in [README.md](README.md).
- [ ] I verified the **canonical link is live** and points to the intended resource.
